School.title: School
School.description: Represents an educational institution within the EduNexus system, serving as the base for school-level users and entities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the School entity. This is the 'School Base' prefix (e.g., 'GBHS1') used in matricule generation.

name.description: The full official name of the school.

code.description: A short, unique code for the school (e.g., 'GBHS').

matriculeObjectId.description: A sequential object ID used in the matricule generation (e.g., '1' for GBHS1).

address.description: The physical address of the school.

contactEmail.description: The primary contact email address for the school.

contactPhone.description: The primary contact phone number for the school.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the school record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the school record was last updated.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account in the EduNexus system, identified by a unique matricule. This entity holds common user information and their role.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity, which is the generated matricule for the user (e.g., 'EduI26CEO001' or 'GBHS126S0001').

email.description: The user's email address, used for login and communication.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

role.description: The role of the user within the system (e.g., 'Platform-CEO', 'School-Admin', 'School-Teacher', 'School-Student').

schoolId.description: Reference to the School this user belongs to, if they are a school-level user. (Relationship: School 1:N User)

matriculeYear.description: The two-digit year (YY) when the user joined the system/school, as part of their matricule.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

StudentProfile.title: StudentProfile
StudentProfile.description: Holds specific profile information for a student user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudentProfile entity, which is the student's matricule (same as User.id).

userId.description: Reference to the associated User entity. (Relationship: User 1:1 StudentProfile)

schoolId.description: Reference to the School the student is enrolled in. (Relationship: School 1:N StudentProfile)

enrollmentDate.description: The date when the student was first enrolled in the school.

gradeLevel.description: The current academic grade level of the student (e.g., 10 for 10th grade).

parentIds.description: References to ParentProfile entities associated with this student. (Relationship: StudentProfile N:N ParentProfile)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the student profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the student profile was last updated.

TeacherProfile.title: TeacherProfile
TeacherProfile.description: Holds specific profile information for a teacher user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TeacherProfile entity, which is the teacher's matricule (same as User.id).

userId.description: Reference to the associated User entity. (Relationship: User 1:1 TeacherProfile)

schoolId.description: Reference to the School the teacher is employed by. (Relationship: School 1:N TeacherProfile)

hireDate.description: The date when the teacher was hired.

department.description: The academic department the teacher belongs to (e.g., 'Mathematics', 'Science').

qualifications.description: A list of academic qualifications or certifications held by the teacher.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the teacher profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the teacher profile was last updated.

StaffProfile.title: StaffProfile
StaffProfile.description: Holds specific profile information for administrative or support staff users (e.g., Sub Admin, BOSA, Librarian).

id.description: Unique identifier for the StaffProfile entity, which is the staff member's matricule (same as User.id).

userId.description: Reference to the associated User entity. (Relationship: User 1:1 StaffProfile)

schoolId.description: Reference to the School the staff member is employed by. (Relationship: School 1:N StaffProfile)

hireDate.description: The date when the staff member was hired.

department.description: The department the staff member works in (e.g., 'Administration', 'Finance', 'Library').

position.description: The specific job title or role of the staff member (e.g., 'Sub Admin', 'Accountant', 'Librarian').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the staff profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the staff profile was last updated.

ParentProfile.title: ParentProfile
ParentProfile.description: Holds specific profile information for a parent or guardian user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ParentProfile entity, which is the parent's matricule (same as User.id).

userId.description: Reference to the associated User entity. (Relationship: User 1:1 ParentProfile)

schoolId.description: Reference to the School their child(ren) attend. (Relationship: School 1:N ParentProfile)

studentIds.description: References to StudentProfile entities linked to this parent. (Relationship: ParentProfile N:N StudentProfile)

relationshipToStudent.description: The relationship of the parent to their child (e.g., 'Father', 'Mother', 'Guardian').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the parent profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the parent profile was last updated.

Course.title: Course
Course.description: Represents a distinct academic subject or curriculum offered by a school.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Course entity.

schoolId.description: Reference to the School offering this course. (Relationship: School 1:N Course)

name.description: The full name of the course (e.g., 'Advanced Algebra').

code.description: A short alphanumeric code for the course (e.g., 'MATH301').

description.description: A brief description of the course content and objectives.

credits.description: The number of academic credits awarded for completing the course.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the course was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the course was last updated.

Class.title: Class
Class.description: Represents a specific offering or section of a Course, typically for a given academic term and taught by a particular teacher.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Class entity.

courseId.description: Reference to the Course this class is an instance of. (Relationship: Course 1:N Class)

teacherId.description: Reference to the TeacherProfile who teaches this class. (Relationship: TeacherProfile 1:N Class)

schoolId.description: Reference to the School offering this class. (Relationship: School 1:N Class)

academicYear.description: The academic year this class is offered (e.g., '2023-2024').

semester.description: The semester or term this class is offered (e.g., 'Fall', 'Spring', 'Summer').

schedule.description: A description of the class meeting times and days (e.g., 'Mon, Wed, Fri 9:00-10:00 AM').

roomNumber.description: The classroom or location where the class meets.

maxCapacity.description: The maximum number of students allowed in this class.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the class record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the class record was last updated.

Enrollment.title: Enrollment
Enrollment.description: Records a student's enrollment in a specific class.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Enrollment entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the StudentProfile who is enrolled. (Relationship: StudentProfile 1:N Enrollment)

classId.description: Reference to the Class the student is enrolled in. (Relationship: Class 1:N Enrollment)

enrollmentDate.description: The date when the student was officially enrolled in the class.

status.description: The current status of the enrollment (e.g., 'Active', 'Completed', 'Dropped', 'Withdrawn').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the enrollment record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the enrollment record was last updated.

Assignment.title: Assignment
Assignment.description: Represents a specific assignment, quiz, exam, or project given to students within a class.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Assignment entity.

classId.description: Reference to the Class this assignment belongs to. (Relationship: Class 1:N Assignment)

title.description: The title or name of the assignment.

description.description: A detailed description of the assignment instructions and requirements.

maxScore.description: The maximum possible score for this assignment.

dueDate.description: The deadline for submitting the assignment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the assignment was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the assignment was last updated.

Grade.title: Grade
Grade.description: Records a student's score and feedback for a specific assignment within a class.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Grade entity.

enrollmentId.description: Reference to the Enrollment record for the student in the class. (Relationship: Enrollment 1:N Grade)

assignmentId.description: Reference to the Assignment this grade is for. (Relationship: Assignment 1:N Grade)

score.description: The score received by the student for the assignment.

feedback.description: Personalized and constructive feedback provided by the teacher for the student's performance on the assignment.

submissionDate.description: The date and time when the assignment was submitted.

gradedByTeacherId.description: Reference to the TeacherProfile who graded this assignment. (Relationship: TeacherProfile 1:N Grade)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the grade record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the grade record was last updated.

ClassSession.title: ClassSession
ClassSession.description: Represents a single scheduled meeting or session of a class.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ClassSession entity.

classId.description: Reference to the Class this session belongs to. (Relationship: Class 1:N ClassSession)

sessionDate.description: The date of the class session.

startTime.description: The start time of the class session.

endTime.description: The end time of the class session.

topicCovered.description: A brief description of the topic(s) covered during the session.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the class session record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the class session record was last updated.

Attendance.title: Attendance
Attendance.description: Records a student's attendance status for a specific class session.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Attendance entity.

enrollmentId.description: Reference to the Enrollment record for the student in the class. (Relationship: Enrollment 1:N Attendance)

classSessionId.description: Reference to the ClassSession this attendance record is for. (Relationship: ClassSession 1:N Attendance)

status.description: The attendance status of the student for the session (e.g., 'Present', 'Absent', 'Late', 'Excused').

recordedByTeacherId.description: Reference to the TeacherProfile who recorded this attendance. (Relationship: TeacherProfile 1:N Attendance)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the attendance record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the attendance record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores UIDs of users with platform-level administrative roles (Founder, CEO, CTO, COO, Investor). Used for Database Access Control (DBAC). Document ID is the Firebase Auth UID. Existence of a document here implies platform-level admin role. Role details are in the /users collection.

params.name: platformAdminUid
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of a platform-level administrator.

definition.description: Stores the last sequential ID for platform-level matricules, indexed by 'YY' (year) and 'ROLE' code (e.g., '26CEO'). Used for atomic matricule generation. Requires transactional updates. No explicit schema provided, assumed a simple counter document.

Main collection for schools. Document ID is the unique School.id (e.g., 'GBHS1'). Includes 'adminUserIds' map (Firebase UIDs) for initial school administration authorization, denormalized for independence.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique identifier for the school (e.g., 'GBHS1').

definition.description: Stores the last sequential ID for school-level matricules, indexed by 'YY' (year) and 'ROLE' code (e.g., '26S'). Unique per school. Requires transactional updates. No explicit schema provided, assumed a simple counter document.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique identifier for the school.

params.name: yearRoleCode
params.description: Combined year and role code (e.g., '26S') for the matricule counter.

definition.description: Stores core user information. Document ID is the Firebase Auth UID. Includes 'matricule' (the unique generated ID like 'GBHS126S0001'), 'role', and 'schoolId' (if applicable) fields which are critical for authorization. Self-read access is allowed.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the user.

definition.description: Stores student-specific profile data. Document ID is the Firebase Auth UID of the student. Includes denormalized 'schoolId' and 'matricule' for authorization and data consistency. Enables easy querying by schoolId for administrative listing (QAPs).

params.name: studentUid
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the student.

definition.description: Stores teacher-specific profile data. Document ID is the Firebase Auth UID of the teacher. Includes denormalized 'schoolId' and 'matricule' for authorization and data consistency. Enables easy querying by schoolId for administrative listing (QAPs).

params.name: teacherUid
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the teacher.

definition.description: Stores staff-specific profile data. Document ID is the Firebase Auth UID of the staff member. Includes denormalized 'schoolId' and 'matricule' for authorization and data consistency. Enables easy querying by schoolId for administrative listing (QAPs).

params.name: staffUid
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the staff member.

definition.description: Stores parent-specific profile data. Document ID is the Firebase Auth UID of the parent. Includes denormalized 'schoolId' and 'matricule' for authorization and data consistency. Enables easy querying by schoolId for administrative listing (QAPs).

params.name: parentUid
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the parent.

definition.description: Courses offered by a specific school. The 'schoolId' is implicitly available for rules from the path. Access control relies on the user's role and school affiliation (from /users) or school admin status (from /schools/{schoolId}.adminUserIds).

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique identifier for the school.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the course.

definition.description: Specific offerings or sections of courses within a school. The 'schoolId' is implicitly available from the path. Includes denormalized 'teacherUserId' (Firebase Auth UID of the assigned teacher) for authorization independence.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique identifier for the school.

params.name: classId
params.description: The unique identifier for the class.

definition.description: Records a student's enrollment in a specific class. Includes denormalized 'studentUserId' (Firebase Auth UID of the enrolled student), 'classId', and 'teacherUserId' (Firebase Auth UID of the class's teacher) for authorization independence and QAPs. SchoolId is in path.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique identifier for the school.

params.name: enrollmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the enrollment record.

definition.description: Represents assignments for classes. Includes denormalized 'classId' and 'teacherUserId' (Firebase Auth UID of the class's teacher) for authorization independence and QAPs. SchoolId is in path.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique identifier for the school.

params.name: assignmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the assignment.

definition.description: Records student scores and feedback for assignments. Includes denormalized 'studentUserId' (Firebase Auth UID), 'teacherUserId' (Firebase Auth UID of the grader/class owner), 'classId', 'assignmentId', and 'enrollmentId' for authorization independence and QAPs. SchoolId is in path.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique identifier for the school.

params.name: gradeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the grade record.

definition.description: Scheduled meetings for a class. Includes denormalized 'classId' and 'teacherUserId' (Firebase Auth UID of the class's teacher) for authorization independence and QAPs. SchoolId is in path.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique identifier for the school.

params.name: classSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the class session.

definition.description: Records student attendance for class sessions. Includes denormalized 'studentUserId' (Firebase Auth UID), 'teacherUserId' (Firebase Auth UID of the recorder/class owner), 'classId', 'classSessionId', and 'enrollmentId' for authorization independence and QAPs. SchoolId is in path.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique identifier for the school.

params.name: attendanceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the attendance record.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for EduNexus prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by strictly adhering to the core design principles and mandates, especially Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
This is the most critical aspect of the design. Traditional hierarchical authorization, where a child document's access depends on fetching data from its parent document, creates brittle security rules and breaks atomic operations. To avoid this, key authorization attributes are denormalized into the documents where they are needed:

1.  **User Context:** Every user's core data, including their `role` and `schoolId` (if applicable), is stored in their `/users/{userId}` document, where `{userId}` is the Firebase Authentication UID. This single source of truth for a user's role and affiliation is fetched once per request context by security rules. However, for any document whose access is *controlled* by a user's `schoolId` or `role`, these fields are further denormalized into the document itself.
2.  **School-Level Data:** For all entities that belong to a specific school (e.g., `Courses`, `Classes`, `Enrollments`, `Assignments`, `Grades`, `Class Sessions`, `Attendance`), the `schoolId` is either explicitly part of the path (e.g., `/schools/{schoolId}/courses/{courseId}`) or denormalized as a field within the document. This ensures that security rules can always determine the school context without relying on `get()` calls to parent documents.
3.  **Collaborative/Relationship Data:** For documents representing relationships or collaborative content (e.g., `Enrollment`, `Assignment`, `Grade`, `Attendance`, `ClassSession`), the relevant `studentUserId` (Firebase UID) and `teacherUserId` (Firebase UID of the teacher responsible for the class/grade/attendance) are denormalized directly into the document. For instance, an `Enrollment` document includes `studentUserId` and `teacherUserId` (of the associated class), allowing students to access their own enrollments and teachers to access their class's enrollments without `get()` calls to the `User` or `Class` documents. Similarly, `Grades` and `Attendance` documents include `studentUserId`, `teacherUserId`, `classId`, and `schoolId` to enable robust, independent access control.

This extensive denormalization ensures that each document contains all necessary information for its own authorization, minimizing `get()` calls in security rules, simplifying rule logic, and preventing race conditions or inconsistent states during atomic operations.

**Structural Segregation for QAPs (Queryable Authorization Patterns):**

The structure facilitates secure `list` operations (QAPs) by segregating data based on its security posture and ownership:

1.  **Platform-Level Roles:** A dedicated `/platform_admins/{platformAdminUid}` collection (where `platformAdminUid` is the Firebase Auth UID) allows for easy, explicit role checks. The existence of a document in this collection grants platform-level admin privileges. This supports QAPs for listing all platform admins and a direct `exists()` check in rules.
2.  **User Profiles:** Core `User` data is in `/users/{userId}`. Specific profiles (`StudentProfile`, `TeacherProfile`, `StaffProfile`, `ParentProfile`) are stored in separate top-level collections (e.g., `/student_profiles/{studentUid}`). Each profile document's ID is the Firebase Auth UID, making path-based ownership rules simple (`match /student_profiles/{studentUid} { allow read: if request.auth.uid == studentUid; }`). Crucially, these profile documents *also* include `schoolId`, enabling School Administrators to easily `list` all students, teachers, staff, or parents within *their* specific school by querying on the `schoolId` field without violating security rules.
3.  **School-Specific Data:** All data tied to a school is organized under `/schools/{schoolId}/...` or as top-level collections with `schoolId` as a field (e.g., `/schools/{schoolId}/enrollments`). This structural segregation ensures that a school's data is logically isolated and access can be easily restricted based on the requesting user's `schoolId` and role. For instance, a teacher can `list` all `Assignments` or `Class Sessions` for their classes by querying `schoolId` and `teacherUserId`. A student can `list` their `Grades` or `Attendance` records by querying `schoolId` and `studentUserId`.

**Matricule System Integration:**

The unique matricule is stored as a field (`matricule`) within the `User` and Profile documents (`User.id`, `StudentProfile.matricule`, `TeacherProfile.matricule`, etc.) while the Firebase Auth UID serves as the document ID for `/users` and all profile collections. This design effectively separates the system's internal identity (`Firebase UID`) used for authorization from the business-level identifier (`matricule`) used for human readability and unique identification, without compromising security rules.

In summary, the design employs radical denormalization of authorization context, clear structural segregation by security posture and ownership, and consistent access modeling patterns. This results in an explicit, easily auditable, and performant Firestore security model that minimizes rule complexity and supports all required QAPs.
